title: Intermittent workflow failures due to resource saturation
symptom: When execution concurrency increases, workflows fail randomly without a clear pattern. This instability is likely linked to resource limitations at the worker level.
root_cause: When the worker processes are under memory pressure, Node.js aggressively triggers garbage collection, which can lead to unstable execution and random failures. Additionally, long-running nodes can block the event loop, exacerbating the issue.
fix_steps: Increase the memory limits for worker processes
Scale horizontally by adding more worker instances
Monitor Redis for stalled jobs and latency
agent_instruction: Increase worker memory limits and add more workers to handle load.
